description: Indicators to analyze for interday (daily bars) market metrics
rationale: Maximized pool of plausible trading signals. Only genuinely non-signal indicators removed (math operators, candlestick patterns, Hilbert transforms, benchmark-dependent). Redundancy handled by market_metrics clustering gate (max 2 per cluster in top-factor lists). Broader pool lets IC analysis discover best-in-cluster per regime.

categories: Math operators and trigonometric functions (20): ADD, DIV, MULT, SUB, ACOS, ASIN, ATAN, CEIL, COS, FLOOR, LN, LOG10, SIN, SQRT, TAN, TANH, AVGPRICE, MEDPRICE, TYPPRICE, WCLPRICE
Exotic candlestick patterns (60): all CDL* indicators — binary signals too sparse for IC analysis
Complex Hilbert transforms (8): HT_* — unstable on finite daily data
Index-returning indicators (4): MAXINDEX, MININDEX, MINMAXINDEX_MIN/MAX — return bar positions not values
Math aggregation operators (5): MAX, MIN, MINMAX_MIN/MAX, SUM — not trading signals
Benchmark-dependent (2): BETA, CORREL — require second series
Variable-period dependent (1): MAVP — requires period array input
Truly identical ROC variants (3): ROCP, ROCR, ROCR100 — mathematically identical IC to ROC
Redundant MACD variants (6): MACDEXT_*, MACDFIX_* — identical to MACD with different defaults
SR zone indicators (4): excluded per design decision
Redundant/unused MAs (8): MA (identical to SMA), WMA, DEMA, TRIMA, MIDPOINT, T3, MAMA, FAMA — core MAs (EMA, SMA, TEMA, KAMA) sufficient; clustering gate handles redundancy

Price-level indicators (5): HIGHEST_HIGH, LOWEST_LOW, BBANDS_UPPER, BBANDS_MIDDLE, BBANDS_LOWER — raw price values with absolute thresholds that become stale; use normalized alternatives (BBANDS_PCT_B, BBANDS_BANDWIDTH)
Price-level regression (3): LINEARREG, LINEARREG_INTERCEPT, TSF — fitted price values; use LINEARREG_SLOPE and LINEARREG_ANGLE for normalized signals
Redundant SAR (1): SAREXT — extended SAR never used, standard SAR sufficient
